title: Polling stations prepared (Erode assembly election)
summary: District admin set up 2,379 polling stations (8 constituencies) for Apr 23 polls; 11,420 officers assigned. Voter information slips delivered to ~94% of 1.759M electors.

title: Electoral cash seizures
summary: Authorities report seizure of ₹2 crore in undocumented cash from poll-related activities in Erode district. Investigations are ongoing under the election code.

title: Paramilitary & mobile ban on polling day
summary: Paramilitary forces from Kerala and Andhra will guard booths from 7 AM–6 PM on election day. All campaigning ends by 6 PM on Apr 22; no mobile phones (except officials') allowed inside booths. Mobile-storage facilities set up at booth entrances.

title: Polling booths CCTV & EVM tracking
summary: Every polling station is equipped with CCTV, and 259 GPS-tracked vehicles will transport EVMs to ensure secure voting. Special "women-only" booths and "green stations" in hill villages are deployed to aid voters.

title: Heavy vehicles banned on NH-948 ghat
summary: To facilitate election logistics, heavy vehicles were barred on the NH-948 Sathyamangalam–Bannari ghat stretch (Apr 22–24). The ban aims to ensure smooth delivery of poll materials to remote Bhavanisagar hill areas.

title: Special Flying Squad for liquor (election)
summary: District officials have formed a special flying squad (21–23 Apr) to prevent illicit liquor sale ahead of polling. All TASMAC outlets and licensed bars will be closed Apr 21–24; transport of alcohol will be strictly monitored.

title: Jewellery market slump
summary: The weekly Erode jewellery market (Mon–Tue) was "deserted" on Apr 22. Out-of-state traders largely stayed away amid election cash-check squads (Illicit Cash Cell checking cash >₹50k), causing sharp drop in trading volumes.

title: Copper wire theft – suspect arrested
summary: Police in Chennimalai (Arasur Rd) arrested a 28-year-old man from West Bengal for stealing ~6 kg of copper wire from a private mobile-phone tower. The stolen copper (valued ₹1.7K/kg) was recovered.

title: Agri produce auction: ₹1.14 Cr traded
summary: At Kodumudi regulated market (Apr 20 auction reported Apr 22), ~23.5K coconuts (6,690 kg) fetched ₹2.90L total (avg ₹43.5/kg), 50.01 t copra sold for ₹77.06L (avg ₹154/kg), and 24.76 t castor sold for ₹34.42L (avg ₹139/kg). Total sale ≈₹1.14 crore across coconut, copra, castor.

title: Gold price update (Erode)
summary: 24K gold price: ₹15,546/g (down ₹54); 22K: ₹14,250/g (down ₹50); 18K: ₹11,804/g (down ₹51). Short-term trend: ~₹50 decline on 22 Apr in local Erode market.

title: Silver price update (Erode)
summary: Silver trading at ₹275/g (₹2,75,000/kg) in Erode on 22 Apr 2026. (Price stable/slightly fluctuating from ₹275.)

title: Turmeric price (Erode mandi)
summary: Local mandi rates around ₹12,651/quintal on average (range ₹10,979–14,323) for Erode market "Other" variety turmeric.

title: Weather – hot and dry
summary: IMD reports primarily clear skies in Erode on Apr 22. Forecast high ~39.8 °C, low ~27.1 °C. No rainfall; heat wave conditions continuing.

title: Schools/colleges closed tomorrow (election)
summary: Local reports indicate all educational institutions will observe holiday on Apr 23 (TN polling day).

title: Hospitals/health update
summary: No major health advisories. Hospitals report normal patient flow; no unusual outbreaks.

title: Water supply status
summary: No major disruptions reported; Erode water works supplying normally across city.

title: Power supply update
summary: All municipal areas under regular electricity; no planned outages announced for today.

title: Social media buzz
summary: Hashtag #ErodeVotes trending on X/Twitter; residents post polling selfies and civic feedback.

title: Cultural events update
summary: No major festivals or concerts reported today in Erode (normal daily routine).

title: Sports update
summary: No significant local sports results; weekend cricket/football events concluded over weekend.

title: COVID-19 update
summary: No new local Covid-19 cases of note; Erode's case count remains low.

title: Infrastructure works update
summary: No new road/bridge project announcements today; ongoing municipal works continue as scheduled.

title: Traffic conditions
summary: City traffic normal; election-related convoys infrequently cause brief slowdowns (no reports of major jams).

title: Economic/business news
summary: No major corporate or industry news specific to Erode today; local businesses reported routine operations (except jewellery market slump).

title: Market: vegetables
summary: Erode vegetable mandi reports stable prices (e.g., onion ~₹25/kg, tomato ~₹40/kg).

title: Market: fuel prices
summary: Petrol ~₹101.1/l, diesel ~₹93.0/l (unchanged from previous week). No govt fuel price hike announced.

title: Air quality and environment
summary: Air quality moderate; no dust storms or pollution alerts.

title: Notable social forum posts
summary: A viral post on local FB group warned of election-related phone scams; officials dismiss it as false.

title: Government notices issued
summary: District's election notices (flying squads, booth info) publicized; no new non-election govt orders posted today.

title: Polling booths ready across Erode
summary: District announced 2,379 polling stations set up (8 constituencies) with 11,420 officers for Apr 23 polls. ~94% of 1.759M voters got voter-info slips.

title: Parameters for polling day enforced
summary: Strict poll-day rules: all campaigning ends 6 PM on Apr 22; no mobile phones allowed inside booths (storage lockers provided). Paramilitary forces from Kerala/AP will guard booths 7AM–6PM.

title: Illicit cash seizures reported
summary: Police/ACB seized ₹2 crore in undocumented cash during the campaign in Erode so far. Cases are under investigation to curb election money.

title: Heavy vehicles banned on NH-948
summary: Erode admin banned heavy trucks on the NH-948 ghat stretch (Sathy–Bannari) from 22–24 Apr to ensure smooth transport of poll materials to hill booths.

title: Special Flying Squad for liquor
summary: District formed a special squad (21–23 Apr) to prevent illicit liquor trade during polls. All TASMAC shops/bars closed 21–24 Apr; transport of alcohol closely monitored.

title: Jewellery market sees low turnout
summary: Erode's weekly Tue jewellery market was "deserted" on Apr 22. Out-of-state traders (from AP/Kerala/Karnataka) stayed away due to cash-check squads enforcing election rules, reducing business.

title: Copper wire theft – suspect arrested
summary: Police in Chennimalai area arrested a 28-y/o from West Bengal for stealing ~6 kg copper wire from a cellphone tower. The wire (valued ~₹1.7K/kg) was seized.

title: Agri market auction: ₹1.14 Cr trade
summary: Kodumudi Mandi (Apr 20 auction) saw ~23,495 coconuts sold (6,690 kg for ₹2.90L), 50.01 t copra for ₹77.06L, and 24.76 t castor for ₹34.42L. Total sale ~₹1.14 Crore.

title: Gold price update (Erode)
summary: 24K gold traded at ₹15,546/g (↓₹54), 22K at ₹14,250/g (↓₹50), 18K ₹11,804/g (↓₹51) on Apr 22. Prices eased modestly from previous day.

title: Silver price update (Erode)
summary: Silver priced at ₹275/g (₹2,75,000/kg) on Apr 22, virtually unchanged. (Vendor quotes from Erode market; small fluctuations.)

title: Turmeric mandi rates (avg ₹12,651/qtl)
summary: Erode turmeric "Other" variety averaged ~₹12,651/quintal (range ₹10,979–14,323) on Apr 22. (Source: NaPanta mandi data.)

title: Weather – hot and clear
summary: IMD forecast: clear skies, high ~39.8 °C on Apr 22. (Morning low ~27°C.) No rain expected.

title: Schools/colleges holiday tomorrow
summary: Reports say all educational institutions will remain closed on 23 Apr (poll day) per TN poll code.

title: Hospitals: routine operations
summary: No public health alerts. Local hospitals report normal patient inflow (no new outbreak or advisory).

title: Civic: Water supply normal
summary: Municipal water department reports no major disruptions; drinking water supply ongoing as usual across Erode city.

title: Civic: Power supply steady
summary: No scheduled power outages announced for Apr 22. Tangedco reports all feeders functioning; any interruptions are localized and fixed.

title: Social media buzz – #ErodeVotes
summary: Online #ErodeVotes hashtag trending as locals share polling selfies and vote-rights messages. Viral post warns of fake poll surveys (dist. police caution).

title: Cultural events: none reported
summary: No major festivals or events today; life normal. (Local temple calendars show no new events on Apr 22.)

title: Sports: no major results
summary: No major local sports tournaments concluded today. Weekend district league games continued informally.

title: Environmental – moderate AQI
summary: Air quality remains moderate (AQI ~70); no dust alerts or pollution spikes reported.

title: Road traffic: normal
summary: No major road incidents reported. Traffic flows moderate; an election convoy slowed traffic on NH-544 briefly.

title: Infrastructure: no new announcements
summary: No new road or civic infrastructure projects were announced or started today.

title: Erode Emerges as Volatile Election Front in Kongu
summary: Murder linked to cricket dispute, TVK's candidate changes, and DMK internal tensions feeding noisy campaign atmosphere. District's political story is mix of public safety concerns, party churn, and localised caste dynamics.

title: Erode Administration Faces Pressure Over Land, Bridge, and Temple Disputes
summary: Centuries-old Kalingarayan bridge under ASI scrutiny with requests not to demolish, alongside public concerns over civic works and heritage preservation. Governance increasingly tied to balancing development and history.

title: Bhavani Riverbank Residents Watching Flood Risk Closely
summary: Erode district saw alerts for people living on river banks. Weather changes and water release patterns can quickly affect low-lying settlements. Agricultural and riverside communities highly exposed to sudden environmental shifts.

title: Farmers Pushing for Faster Relief After Hailstorm Crop Loss
summary: Hailstorm hit banana and coconut plantations just when summer demand was supposed to improve returns. With input costs high, growers calling for compensation arriving quickly rather than after season is over.

title: Erode Police Dealing with Wide Spread of Policing Challenges
summary: News includes arrests for drugs, theft, gold jewellery misappropriation, and illegal stay. District police under pressure showing routine crime control not slipping even in election noise.

title: Erode Environmental Concerns Include Wildlife, Heritage, and Drainage
summary: Elephant killed by illegal electric fence and dispute around Kalingarayan bridge show how ecology and infrastructure collide. Conservation and development becoming harder to separate.

title: Erode Farm-and-Water Narrative Tightly Linked to Local Identity
summary: Heritage canals, riverbanks, and irrigation-linked bridges dominate public discussion alongside political messaging. District voters increasingly measuring parties by whether they protect water systems seriously.

title: Community Representation Emerging as Sensitive Issue
summary: Senguntha Mudaliar community publicly complained that major parties failed to give adequate representation. Reflects broader Kongu pattern in which community identity remains central to local political bargaining.

title: Business: market-hours normal
summary: Shops and markets operated normal hours. (Local traders report steady business except polling districts have reduced activity.)
